2606.15206.title: AI Contagion in Social Networks
2606.15206.paper_summary: We study how artificial intelligence (AI) interacts with social communication networks to shape the stability of collective knowledge. Agents exchange information through a network while receiving AI-generated content, and AI systems retrain on the aggregate social information they influence. This interaction generates two feedback forces: an AI contagion channel, through which distortions diffuse across the network, and an AI social distortion multiplier, through which retraining amplifies past errors. Despite the high dimensionality of the environment, we show that the long-run behavior of the system admits a two-dimensional representation whose spectral radius determines whether AI-mediated information systems are dynamically stable or unstable. We characterize a sharp regulatory frontier identifying the minimum filtering required for stability and show how network topology shapes systemic informational risk.
2606.15206.paper_summary_zh: 我們研究人工智慧（AI）如何與社交通信網絡互動，以塑造集體知識的穩定性。<lb/>代理通過網絡交換信息，同時接收AI生成的內容，而AI系統則在其影響的社會信息總體上進行再訓練。<lb/>這種互動產生了兩種反饋力量：一個是AI傳染通道，通過它，扭曲在網絡中擴散；另一個是AI社會扭曲乘數，通過它，再訓練放大了過去的錯誤。<lb/>儘管環境的高維度性，我們顯示系統的長期行為可以用二維表示，該表示的譜半徑決定了AI介導的信息系統是動態穩定還是不穩定。<lb/>我們界定了一個明確的監管邊界，確定了穩定所需的最小過濾，並展示了網絡拓撲如何塑造系統性信息風險。
2606.15206.author: Olivier Bos et.al.
